title: coronopus integrifolius
intro: Coronopus integrifolius, also known as sea purslane or glasswort, is a succulent, annual or biennial plant native to the coastal areas of Europe, Asia, and Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Coronopus integrifolius is rich in vitamins A and C, potassium, and other essential minerals, providing various health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-aging, and immune-boosting effects. The plant can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, or used in salves and creams to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
